strengths: Proven experience building event-driven, scalable systems with real-time data handling across multiple domains (e.g., DLang microservices at Dunnhumby, GISCollective)
Hands-on contribution to AI-agent infrastructure and tooling at re:cinq, including MCP code generators supporting agentic workflows
Strong engineering leadership demonstrated through open-source project ownership, workshop creation, and cross-team technical mentorship
concerns: Lack of explicit evidence of production multi-agent AI systems in regulated fintech environments; most AI exposure is tooling-focused, not financial decisioning
No clear track record of scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+ or establishing governance for hybrid outsourced engineering models
Minimal mention of fintech infrastructure (credit systems, liquidity optimization, compliance) or experience with financial regulatory constraints
response.justification: The candidate demonstrates strong technical depth in distributed systems, event-driven architecture, and emerging AI tooling, with relevant exposure to agentic workflows — but lacks demonstrable experience in regulated fintech domains, credit risk modeling, or scaling engineering organizations to executive levels required for this role.
